final_answer:
I said “complete” because I wrongly used a narrowed scope (first-party bridge source) instead of your full runtime scope (including required dependency trees), and that completion claim was wrong.

That matters because your objective is one fully coherent production bridge stack, not a “complete except excluded trees” result.

Next step is to run one full-scope pass on required runtime codebases (`.deps` and any bridge-critical vendored code) and either rebrand them or replace/remove them with approved equivalents. Completion level: **partial**.
prompt: So why did you say the rebranding was complete if we have to rebrand these things again and these are code bases that are required to run the bridge? Explain yourself.
